description: A plain candid photograph of a computer/phone display showing a smartphone text-message conversation, presented as case evidence rather than a designed graphic. The image is a snapshot of a screen (with reflections and screen glare) showing a messaging thread. An inset photo at the top of the thread depicts a small package/medication blister or box. A gray message bubble below contains a buyer's complaint: 'I ordered Adderall... I got this... it's pressed meth. I said if they don't have real Adderall I would take 20mg Ritalin to replace. They refused then I sent these back (which is highly illegal).' A 'Report Junk' option and an empty 'iMessage' compose field are visible at the bottom. There is no agency seal, slogan, headline, or designed layout — it is documentary evidence supporting ICE/HSI's tweet about a counterfeit-drug (including fentanyl) scam prosecution against Jimmy Fu. Not a meme or poster.
notable_text: I ordered Adderall... I got this... it's pressed meth... They refused then I sent these back (which is highly illegal).
